place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast area and wanted to share my thoughts on East Coast Park. It remains one of the best-maintained spaces in Singapore, and its extensive connectivity to other parks makes it an ideal destination for long walks or cycling trips. I particularly enjoyed the ability to ride my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay via the park connector; it was always such a calm and refreshing experience.

The park offers a wonderful atmosphere with its blend of lush greenery and sea views. It is exceptionally well-equipped for families and fitness enthusiasts, featuring dedicated exercise stations, children’s play areas, and wide, separate paths for cycling and walking that prevent the space from feeling crowded.

Beyond the scenery, the facilities are excellent. There are convenient bicycle rentals, a variety of dining options in the restaurant area where you can enjoy the sea breeze, and well-maintained BBQ pits and shelters. For those interested in staying longer, the designated camping areas are a great touch, and I’ve always found the washrooms and bathing facilities to be very clean.

Overall, it is a perfect place to relax, exercise, and enjoy nature, whether you are visiting alone or with friends and family.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast Park is one of the best and most well-maintained parks in Singapore. It is a very big park and is beautifully connected to other parks across Singapore, making it perfect for long walks or cycling trips. The park has lots of nice scenery, with greenery and sea views that make the atmosphere very relaxing. In some areas, there are exercise equipment stations and children’s play areas, which are great for families and fitness lovers.

There is a dedicated cycling path and a separate walking path, both of which are wide and well maintained, so it never feels crowded. You can also rent bicycles, and there are fun activities and attractions that bring in many people. The park has a restaurant area where you can enjoy a variety of foods while feeling the cool sea breeze. You can book BBQ pits, and there are huts available for shelter in case of rain. There are also designated camping areas for those who want to stay overnight.

I used to live near the East Coast area, and what I loved most was riding my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay, as the park connector makes this possible. It was always a calm and refreshing experience. On top of that, the washrooms and bathing areas are very clean and well maintained. Overall, East Coast Park is a perfect place to relax, exercise, eat, and enjoy nature, whether alone, with friends, or with family.

review_text:
This is one of the most beautiful parks I’ve ever visited in Singapore.

It has plenty of trees, clean walking paths, and comfortable benches where people can relax and enjoy the peaceful atmosphere. I especially recommend visiting in the morning because the weather is cooler and the park feels very refreshing

There are also many trash bins around the park, so it is convenient for visitors who bring drinks or snacks. The area is well maintained, clean, and perfect for taking a walk, reading a book, or simply spending quiet time away from the busy city. 🌳

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
First time trying A Hot Hideout at Kinex, and I can see why there’s always a long queue whenever I walk past. Finally gave it a try and it didn’t disappoint. Ordering was quite straightforward with clear step-by-step instructions, so it was easy even for first-timers.

We ordered the dry mala and tomato broth. The mala was nicely flavoured and not too overpowering, which I liked. The tomato soup was rich and flavourful, not the bland kind.

Adding toppings like scrambled egg really made a difference and enhanced both dishes.
The green Thai milk tea was also not very sweet, which was a plus and paired well with the meal. The highlight for me was the fried lotus root—super crispy and went really well with the dry mala and scrambled egg combo.

Overall, a very satisfying meal and a good experience. Will definitely come back again.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Mala xiang guo restaurant located in 1st floor of kinex mall.

Food: Delicious. Had not had a delicious tasting mala xiang guo in a along time. The ingredients don't taste wet and boiled like the other stalls you find in hawker center and food court. It's dry and mala flavoured well mixed in. Topped with creamy scrambled egg, the texture of the well cooked instant noodle and chewy potato noodle makes me not want to stop eating.

The lotus root and potato were sliced and fried. Its a good combo to dip the fried item in their secret powder.

Service: Staff was very prompt to help with the ordering process which is not complicated. Food was also served quickly.

Atmosphere: Air conditioned with lots of seating. Place was also clean and tidy.

Only feedback I have is for the lotus root and potato to be sliced to small portions. They are huge for a 1 person portion.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Food was great. Had the mala collagen soup (medium spicy) it was “ma” 麻 and “la” 辣, not jus the overpowering spiciness.
Iced tea was also very good.
The cost depends on the amount of ingredients you pick.
Service given 4⭐️ because everything was self-serve and collect. But the staff was helpful for me, a first timer.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Best and comfy atmosphere. The space is spacious. The staff is always ready to help even with self check out. My most preferred dine in place.

Food has good wok hey.... and free flow of special sauce, peanut, spring onion, coriander and fragrant powder. The service stands out with good cleanliness.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I would like to start on complimenting the staffs! They are very nice and accommodating! They give the service that you need over and beyond. And the place is so clean, organise and tidy! FOOD WAS HEAVEN TO ME! I tried the collagen soup and it’s very comforting! I don’t usually get to taste this kind of soup in other places here in SG. Definitely this is now my go to now !  The mantou was perfectly cooked . The crunch and softness even it’s not hot anymore was still there! . HOPE THEY GET TO THE SINGAPORE BEST FOOD.  Also the price is not so bad ! It’s good and it won’t break your bank!

review_text: This is a large swimming complex and great for kids on the 5th floor. There are 3 pools. One for small kiddies, one intermediate and one advanced. Pool is not heated but good for warm weather days. The Olympic pool has a shelter. There is an activesg gym on the 6th floor that overlooks the pool if you want to work out (separate entrance fee of 2.50 sgd). However, the gym are strict with footwear and need to bring your own towel.

review_text:
Our Tampines Hub (OTH) continues to be one of the most vibrant community spaces in Singapore. Even on a late weekday evening, the energy here is unmistakable. Here’s a review from my recent 8:30 PM visit.

🏮 Festive Vibes: Chinese New Year 2026

The Hub is currently decked out for Chinese New Year, and the atmosphere is fantastic.

• CNY Activities: There are plenty of festive displays and activities scattered throughout the ground floor, from zodiac-themed decorations to cultural showcases. It adds a wonderful touch of tradition to the modern architecture.

• The Crowd: Even at 8:30 PM, the place was quite crowded. It clearly remains the "heart" of Tampines, with families and residents soaking in the festive spirit late into the evening.

🎬 Entertainment: The Arena & Festive Plaza

One of the best features of OTH is the dedicated space for public viewing.

• Live Performances & Movies: The Festive Plaza (the large open atrium) is essentially an arena for the community. There’s almost always something on the "Big Ass Screen"—whether it's a live performance, a football match, or a free movie screening. It’s a great spot to just sit on the steps and unwind.

• The "Big Ass Fans": Despite being an open-air
concept, the cooling is impressive. The massive HVLS (High Volume Low Speed) fans—or "big ass fans" as many call them—keep the air circulating beautifully. Even in our humid weather, the breeze makes the space comfortable for hanging out.

🍛 Food & Convenience

If you're feeling hungry, you're never more than a few steps away from a good meal.

• The Hawker Centre: Located right next to the main festive areas, the OTH Hawker Centre is the perfect pitstop. It offers a massive variety of halal and non-halal stalls, making it incredibly convenient for a quick dinner after catching a show or finishing a workout.

Final Verdict

Our Tampines Hub is the gold standard for community spaces. Between the festive CNY activities and the breezy, comfortable "arena" area, it’s a great place to spend an evening.

I haven’t tried their food yet, but I’ve heard good things about it, so I’m looking forward to giving it a try next time.

The environment at Our Tampines Hub is also really nice and lively where there a lot of chairs area . I like the hawker area a lot, especially the seafood stalls all the way at the back it has a good variety

Overall, it’s a chill place with a nice vibe, good service, and a lot of food options around.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Tampines Hub (OTH) is truly an excellent community hub with a wide range of amenities under one roof. From the food court and sports facilities to live performances and a one-stop service centre, it offers great convenience for residents. The ample parking spaces also make visits hassle-free. A well-planned hub that caters to people of all ages and interests.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Our Tampines Hub truly feels like the heartbeat of the community, and it’s easy to understand why it’s known as Singapore’s largest community and lifestyle centre. The scale is impressive, but more than that, it reflects Singapore’s resilience and commitment to supporting community living in a meaningful way.

The architecture is beautiful, modern, and thoughtfully designed with sustainability in mind. Everything is conveniently housed in one place — hawker centres, food courts, restaurants, learning centres, indoor and outdoor running tracks, rooftop gardens, and sky gardens. The layout makes it easy to spend hours here without feeling overwhelmed, whether you’re running errands or just hanging out.

One of the standout highlights is the large central screen with seating around it. It creates a fantastic shared atmosphere for concerts, movie screenings, or watching live-streamed matches, especially when Singapore is being represented at major events like the Olympics. There’s also a football court that regularly hosts matches and other events, adding to the vibrant energy of the space.

Families are well catered for too, with a climbing wall and dedicated play areas for children. The large auditorium, which seats around 1,500 people, is another impressive feature, often hosting performances and community events. Essential government services like Service SG are also located here, making it incredibly convenient. Even though it gets busy in the evenings, the lively footfall just shows how well-loved and actively used Our Tampines Hub really is.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
📍Level 2, Our Tampines Hub

👏🏻 Sheltered playground alert 👏🏻

Ah, this one holds a special place in our heart as my kid grew up at this playground. From stumbling around to confidently running full tilt at the trampoline now.

👧🏻 Toddler verdict: 8/10. The shelter is a big plus point in our tropical weather. This huge area is padded and comes complete with many toddler-friendly zones

👉 Parents’ note: There’s one more discreet (and sheltered) playground at level 4 if this one gets crowded

Follow us @slideseekersg on Instagram for more playground reviews!

• The Crowd: Even at 8:30 PM, the place was quite crowded. It clearly remains the "heart" of Tampines, with families and residents soaking in the festive spirit late into the evening.

🎬 Entertainment: The Arena & Festive Plaza

One of the best features of OTH is the dedicated space for public viewing.

• Live Performances & Movies: The Festive Plaza (the large open atrium) is essentially an arena for the community. There’s almost always something on the "Big Ass Screen"—whether it's a live performance, a football match, or a free movie screening. It’s a great spot to just sit on the steps and unwind.

• The "Big Ass Fans": Despite being an open-air
concept, the cooling is impressive. The massive HVLS (High Volume Low Speed) fans—or "big ass fans" as many call them—keep the air circulating beautifully. Even in our humid weather, the breeze makes the space comfortable for hanging out.

🍛 Food & Convenience

If you're feeling hungry, you're never more than a few steps away from a good meal.

• The Hawker Centre: Located right next to the main festive areas, the OTH Hawker Centre is the perfect pitstop. It offers a massive variety of halal and non-halal stalls, making it incredibly convenient for a quick dinner after catching a show or finishing a workout.

Final Verdict

Our Tampines Hub is the gold standard for community spaces. Between the festive CNY activities and the breezy, comfortable "arena" area, it’s a great place to spend an evening.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Tampines Hub (OTH) is truly an excellent community hub with a wide range of amenities under one roof. From the food court and sports facilities to live performances and a one-stop service centre, it offers great convenience for residents. The ample parking spaces also make visits hassle-free. A well-planned hub that caters to people of all ages and interests.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Our Tampines Hub truly feels like the heartbeat of the community, and it’s easy to understand why it’s known as Singapore’s largest community and lifestyle centre. The scale is impressive, but more than that, it reflects Singapore’s resilience and commitment to supporting community living in a meaningful way.

The architecture is beautiful, modern, and thoughtfully designed with sustainability in mind. Everything is conveniently housed in one place — hawker centres, food courts, restaurants, learning centres, indoor and outdoor running tracks, rooftop gardens, and sky gardens. The layout makes it easy to spend hours here without feeling overwhelmed, whether you’re running errands or just hanging out.

One of the standout highlights is the large central screen with seating around it. It creates a fantastic shared atmosphere for concerts, movie screenings, or watching live-streamed matches, especially when Singapore is being represented at major events like the Olympics. There’s also a football court that regularly hosts matches and other events, adding to the vibrant energy of the space.

Families are well catered for too, with a climbing wall and dedicated play areas for children. The large auditorium, which seats around 1,500 people, is another impressive feature, often hosting performances and community events. Essential government services like Service SG are also located here, making it incredibly convenient. Even though it gets busy in the evenings, the lively footfall just shows how well-loved and actively used Our Tampines Hub really is.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
✔️ Review of Hawkers' Street @ Eastpoint Mall Wok Hei Hor Fun (Michelin Bib Gourmand)

Location:
Hawkers Street, Stall no. 8
3 Simei Street 6, Level 3, Singapore 528833

A popular Singaporean dish featuring flat rice noodles stir-fried over high heat to create a smoky, charred flavour called wok hei. Usually served with beef or fish in a savory, prawns, egg-based gravy, known for tender meat and smooth noodles.

My first visit to this stall at Hawker Street located at East Point Mall was last week, on Saturday, February 28, 2026. The taste was okay but lacked the signature wok hei flavour; it felt like regular hor fun, similar to what I usually have for takeaway or eat at hawkers.

The original, famous stall is at Redhill Hawker Centre, which has been awarded the Michelin Bib Gourmand for their smoky hor fun with prawns.

Quote
“Wok Hei Hor Fun (Redhill Original
Stall)
85 Redhill Ln, #01-94 Redhill
Food Centre, Singapore
150085
Located in: Redhill Food Centre
Unquote

The wok hei flavour is strong and lingering, providing a smoky aroma, and the sauce is flavourful and comforting.

I was recommended the Assorted Hor Fun ($6.90), which includes sliced fish (soft and fresh), tender pork, and plump prawns (not the freshest). The noodles (Kuey Teow) are uneven, lacking enough wok hei because they didn't use sufficient heat for a smoky, charred flavour. This could be improved.

I will try another dish with wok hei again on my next trip. I would like to thank the cook, Xue Lu, and his colleague Joanne for their effort in preparing the meal. There is room for improvement. There may still be some variation in the quality of dishes among different locations as they strive to attract more customers.

Nevertheless, Keep improving the quality of the dish. Customers will return for the best Wok Hey Hor Fun in the east!

Chris
2 Mar 2026

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Met an extremely helpful young man working at Tai Seng Mrt station by the name of Kai Yong. He spent a fair bit of time explaining to me how to get to my destination due to the underground works affecting the train direction at the moment. Super amazing service! I’m deeply impressed

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I want to compliment a crowd marshal by the name of Ricardo! He is a perfect example of excellent service where he rendered a great help to me just now by giving me an accurate and precise direction for me when I got lost in the station! He is very patient in his explanation and is always smiling even though i threw temple at him initially due to my flustered manner! He even helped me to carry my heavy luggage up the escalator as there isn't any lift at the exit.
Nevertheless I am very blessed to have met him and many thanks to him so that I can meet up with my family. Please help me to extend my thanks to him and I hope there are more such crowd marshals like him.
Thank you Ricardo and all at Tai Seng Mrt station!

key amenities at taiseng station

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Worst train station to be at during peak hours. At around 6-640pm, you can’t even get on the train, especially towards Bishan. Be prepared to wait for at least 3-4 trains during that time. People will just push their way in just to board the train.
Beware. But some hack for anyone who read this, there will be an empty train at 621pm. You can board that train to avoid the squeezing.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Review of ✔️NLB Public Library Tampines Regional at Our Tampines Hub (OTH) 👍

On November 18, 2025, I had the opportunity to visit the large community centre, Our Tampines Hub (OTH), located in Tampines. During my visit, I explored the NLB Public Library Tampines Regional, which is conveniently situated inside the building.

I was pleased to find out that the library is quite spacious, spanning five levels from the 2nd to the 6th floor, and providing services for residents of all ages, from children to adults.

This venue is an excellent place for studying and will particularly appeal to book lovers. The library features numerous chairs and tables, ensuring ample seating at all times. It is an exceptionally quiet environment, making sounds from the children's section barely noticeable.

The indoor kids' playground is soundproof, allowing children to play freely. Parents can bring their children here with confidence, as staff are present to supervise and ensure their safety. Wonderful!

Members can also reserve seats in advance through an online booking system, with a maximum reservation period of four hours. Same-day reservations are available, except during busy times such as the start of school exams like the GCE O and A Levels, when the library becomes very crowded with students.

I thoroughly enjoyed my time there; it is a wonderful place to explore. We recently relocated to this area.

The residents of Tampines are indeed fortunate to have access to such a large, immensely popular library that is not only widely used but also deeply cherished.

Welcome to the Tampines Public Library!

Chris
21 Nov 2025

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Step into a space where curiosity thrives and knowledge knows no bounds. The Tampines Library isn’t just a building of books—it’s an inviting haven designed to spark learning and inspire deeper understanding of everything under the sky.
I dropped by on a weekday to look up some research and reference materials, and was amazed at how thoughtfully each level has been designed. Every corner draws you in, encouraging visitors to immerse themselves in discovery, reflection, and growth.
Whether you’re here for study, inspiration, or a quiet escape, this library offers a spectrum of possibilities waiting for you to explore.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
One of the best libraries in Singapore, hands down. This place is more than just a library it’s a full lifestyle and learning hub.
Spread across five levels, the space is huge, modern, and thoughtfully designed for everyone from kids to working adults. The environment is clean, comfortable, and perfect whether you want to study, read, or just relax.

What really impressed me is the variety of facilities from quiet study areas and cosy reading corners to tech spaces like makerspaces and even an indoor playground for kids. It truly caters to all ages and interests.

The book collection is extensive, and the self-service borrowing and return system is super efficient and easy to use. Many spots also offer great views of the surrounding stadium, making the whole experience even more enjoyable.

Only downside is that it can get quite crowded during weekends, but that just shows how popular and well-loved this place is.

Highly recommended if you’re looking for a productive study spot, a peaceful escape, or even a place to bring your family

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Tampines Library is located on the second floor of Tampines Hub. One thing I noticed is that it has two entrances – one of them is nearer to the eateries, which is super convenient if you want to grab a quick bite before or after studying.

The study corner is a bit limited in terms of seating, but almost all seats come with a power plug, which is really handy. The library is also well maintained and clean. Overall, a good place to study if you can grab a spot early.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Located within the Our Tampines Hub or OTH, this library has 5 levels that cater to all ages. From L2 for the little ones to L6 for more matured residents. Access to all levels via escalators or lifts with modern facilities.

The "Tampinesians" are very lucky and it was pretty crowded on the Sunday I was there and looks like it is widely used and popular.

review_text:
Review of ✔️NLB Public Library Tampines Regional at Our Tampines Hub (OTH) 👍

On November 18, 2025, I had the opportunity to visit the large community centre, Our Tampines Hub (OTH), located in Tampines. During my visit, I explored the NLB Public Library Tampines Regional, which is conveniently situated inside the building.

I was pleased to find out that the library is quite spacious, spanning five levels from the 2nd to the 6th floor, and providing services for residents of all ages, from children to adults.

This venue is an excellent place for studying and will particularly appeal to book lovers. The library features numerous chairs and tables, ensuring ample seating at all times. It is an exceptionally quiet environment, making sounds from the children's section barely noticeable.

The indoor kids' playground is soundproof, allowing children to play freely. Parents can bring their children here with confidence, as staff are present to supervise and ensure their safety. Wonderful!

Members can also reserve seats in advance through an online booking system, with a maximum reservation period of four hours. Same-day reservations are available, except during busy times such as the start of school exams like the GCE O and A Levels, when the library becomes very crowded with students.

I thoroughly enjoyed my time there; it is a wonderful place to explore. We recently relocated to this area.

The residents of Tampines are indeed fortunate to have access to such a large, immensely popular library that is not only widely used but also deeply cherished.

Welcome to the Tampines Public Library!

Chris
21 Nov 2025

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Geylang Serai Market and Food Centre
review_text: fantastic family outing on a Sunday morning; bustling atmosphere, and authentic local energy. The upstairs food court is a standout, serving a wide variety of delicious dishes. Added benefit of clean, well-maintained facilities.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Geylang Serai Market and Food Centre
review_text:
It has a great collection of Malaysian and halal foods with some of the stalls being frequently mentioned in my food research as some of the best. There's also a market on the ground floor and what looked like some clothing shops next to the hawker center on the second floor.
It's also a cool hawker center to check out because it wasn't touristy at all. I was the only obvious tourist I saw there but I got there about an hour before it closed so maybe it's different earlier in the day, I dunno.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Geylang Serai Market and Food Centre
review_text: One of the oldest markets in Singapore. Now, very nicely renovated. It has two sections: one is the agri-food market, the other is a hawker center where you can eat cheaply and well.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Geylang Serai Market and Food Centre
review_text: One of the most authentic wet markets and hawker centres, you can get the most variety of fresh vegetables and spices available. And prices are one of the most competitive and reasonable options available. Check out their 2nd level food selection and also sundries and clothing section for the traditional and trending fashion of the Malay racial diversity.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: attended a 3 day wake at the oratory of the Church of Nativity recently. On one of the evenings, I went for a walk in the park. It was pleasant to be in nature, to reflect on the life. The park was however crowded in the evening with people exercising, cycling. walking their dogs and having picnics. Love the trees.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
It's a mind-blowing park for spending time with nature ,any time can go and spend time in peace , best place for morning time exercises and walking or running , meditation etc etc . Early in the morning the feeling here is very nice and quiet , the public toilet is also here ,
One wedding machine is also there to take your favourite drinks .

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Even though this library is not as big as Woodlands and Jurong East Regional Libraries, it still carries plenty of books and it’s spacious enough too with plenty of comfortable seats.
I also like the more relaxed vibe here with plenty of plants.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

I wish that the Geylang East Library was closer to where I stayed so I could go there more often. The library has a nice location because it’s just 5 minutes away from the MRT. The library has 2 levels. On the first level there is the borrowing books section, and right across the hall there is a children books section. The children’s section is one of the best children’s sections I’ve seen. When I had walked in I noticed immediately that the room was not tiny. The children’s section is a good size for children because some children are very hyper, so if they want to find books that they are interested in they can just keep walking around because the room is a very nice size, and find books. The second level has Cooking, Singapore Collection, Fiction, Non-fiction, Business, and YP (Young People) books. I like it when libraries have books on display so I can maybe browse through them and that’s what the Geylang library had. There’s also this nice long sofa that multiple people can sit on and also a nice place to use a computer on. There are also a few seats and smaller tables in the middle close to the sofa and that’s really nice for having a pile of books and just reading for the day. Overall, I think the Geylang East library is a very nice place to study, use your computer, and read for the whole day if you’re up for it.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Air quality in this library is really bad. I don’t think it’s suitable for kids/teens. Carpet floor collects so much dust, should be replaced. This is my go to library in my pri school days, but now I come out of it having a sore throat due to a sensitive nose

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Bedok Mall is the vibrant heart of the East, offering an incredibly convenient and comprehensive shopping experience for both locals and visitors. What I love most about this mall is its seamless integration with the Bedok MRT station and the bus interchange, making it exceptionally easy to access. Despite its compact size compared to downtown malls, it is packed with a fantastic variety of retail shops, supermarkets, and dining options that cater to every taste and budget. It has a wonderful community feel—it’s always bustling with energy but remains very family-friendly and well-organized. Whether you are looking for trendy fashion, quick daily necessities at the supermarket, or a great meal at one of the many popular restaurants, Bedok Mall has it all under one roof. The layout is intuitive, making it a breeze to navigate even during the busy weekend rush. It’s truly a one-stop destination that perfectly serves the needs of the neighborhood. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a convenient and fulfilling shopping trip in the East of Singapore!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
PLQ Mall – The Standout Mall of the Paya Lebar Area
10 Paya Lebar Road, Singapore 409057
Having spent considerable time in the Paya Lebar area across multiple Singapore visits, PLQ Mall has consistently proven itself to be the best shopping and dining destination the neighbourhood has to offer — and a personal firm favourite.
The food options are a particular highlight, with an impressive selection of dining choices spread across the upper levels, building anticipation as you ride the escalators upward. The top floor food court is especially noteworthy — well stocked with a wide variety of cuisines, clean, and comfortably laid out, making it an easy choice for any meal of the day.
Beyond dining, the mall's connectivity is a genuine strength — conveniently linked to nearby hotels and within easy reach of the MRT station, making it an effortlessly practical base for visitors and locals alike.
For anyone staying in or passing through the Paya Lebar area, PLQ Mall is an easy and enthusiastic first recommendation.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A mall that provides wide variety of food choices. Big  outdoor space between buildings can be use for event, temporary pop up for different seasons. A very lively mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: PLQ Mall is a vibrant and convenient shopping destination designed by DP Architects. The mall offers a great mix of shops, restaurants, and cafes, with modern, well-thought-out spaces. Its location by Paya Lebar MRT makes it super accessible. Highly recommended for families and urban explorers!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: I’m a little confused, are they connected or I do not where I was? Followed my Queen as she brought me here to explore a corner of Singapore I have never seen before. We had Onkei Tonkatsu and Ramen Keisuke here

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Four floors n two basements of shopping pleasure! Great restaurants, children-related stores, kopitiam and NTUC supermarket plus many more varieties.  It's a wonderful,  friendly environment. Third floor has a number of great restaurants like Tung Lok and Haidilao.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Old Airport Road Food Centre exceeded my expectations with a great variety of Singapore heritage dishes. I also loved the creativity behind many stall names referencing places like Chong Pang, Kallang, and Blanco Court — adds local charm.

• Old Airport Road Fried Hokkien Mee (#01-99) – 4/5: Wet-style Hokkien Mee with decent wok hei, generous prawns, good value.

• Famous Old Airport Fried Oyster (#01-101) – 3/5: Soggy omelette, overcooked prawns, not worth $10.

• Chong Pang Huat BBQ (#01-90) – 3/5: Expensive, small portions, hygiene issues.

• Jin Fried Rice (#01-63) – 5/5: Fragrant mala fried rice with pork chop — excellent, must-try.

• Chuan Loo Bee Hokkien Food (#01-111) – 2/5: Tough pork ribs, odd sauce, disappointing.

• Whampoa Soya Bean (#01-68) – 4/5: Solid traditional desserts; soya drinks slightly diluted.

• Unkai Japanese Cuisine (#01-88) – 5/5: Generous salmon bowl, wallet-friendly, satisfying.

• Western Barbeque (#01-53) – 3/5: Chicken chop okay but lacks flavour; queues are long.

• Famous Kallang Airport Fried Oyster (#01-55) – 2/5: Oily, overwhelming, still hunting for a better version.

• Salt (#01-128) – 4/5: Aglio Olio with teriyaki chicken — decent but slightly pricey.

• Yi Ji Fried Prawn Noodles (#01-73) – 3.5/5: Carrot cake decent, noodles not outstanding.

• Xin Mei Xiang Lor Mee (#01-116) – 4/5: Double Fish Lor Mee decent; broth mediocre, some service issues.

• Heritage Tanglin Puff – 5/5: Singapore institution since 1952. Freshly fried puffs with generous fillings — curry potato and curry chicken are standouts. 6 for $12, great value.

• Ah Yee Hong Kong Roasted (#01-77) – 4/5: Three-meat combo rice ($10) — char siew well caramelised, roast pork had satisfying crackling, but roast duck was chewy and rice was dry. Chilli a nice touch. Would love a chicken option on the menu. Worth a visit for the char siew and roast pork.

Overall, a must-visit for variety and heritage flavours, though some stalls are hit-or-miss. Will keep exploring and updating!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Old Airport Road Food Center is one of popular places for breakfast and lunch.  There are many nice food around such as Lor Mee, Hokkien Mee, Prawn Noddle, Chicken Rice, Roasted Pork and Duck, Western Food, Wanton Noddle.  There is also a new NTUC at level 2.  This food Center can be quite packed during peaked hours.  If you are around Kallang area, I will recommend you guys to drop by …

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Great hawker centre but it can get a little stuffy. Try to grab one of the round tables by the entrances or near the ventilation areas for more space and cool air.

Loved the lor mee! Char koay teow and prawn mee we’re decent too.

If you’re getting ais kacang or cendol, get it from the popular stall in the middle to avoid disappointment!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Very nice and patient uncle who runs this stall. I always thought sugar canes all tasted the same, until I tried this. Happened to find a seat in front of this stall and decided to just buy from him. The sugar cane was really fresh and had a clean taste (there was not that sugary aftertaste). Uncle was also telling me that his is not soaked in sugar syrup and yet it was still naturally sweet! Highly recommended!

I think $2 for a cup, extra for lemon.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: One of my favourite food centres. Great selection and food. Easy to get to via Bus 36 from Changi. Due for a nice power cleaning though.

Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

review_text: I like this mall. It isn't a typical shopping mall where one goes to shop. This mall caters to sports enthusiasts given its location. review_text:
A nice, vibrant place with a good mix of shopping, dining, and activities. It's conveniently located next to the Stadium MRT, making it easily accessible. The mall has a relaxed and spacious atmosphere, and it’s never too crowded, which makes walking around very comfortable.

There are a variety of stores to explore, from daily essentials to fashion outlets. Dining options are plenty, with cafes, restaurants, and a food court offering many choices. There’s also Climb Central for indoor rock climbing and Splash-N-Surf for kids, making it great for families and those looking for active fun.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Love this lib. Looks so new and design so contemporary. As with all the new libraries, the design is thoughtful with good reading areas, working areas, charging points. These days I love going to the library because it is such an enjoyment experience. Nestled within heartbeat@bedok with supermarket and f&b. Building has its own car park. Water dispenser avail just outside lib. Lib spread two floors with internal staircase.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I was surprised to find tourist reviews for such a niche place! This must be the kind of spot people visit only if they’re on an extended trip to Singapore and have the time to explore a community library with no real scenic appeal.

The library is located on the 2nd and 3rd floors of the community center (CC). Taking the escalator is much faster since the cylindrical lift is extremely inefficient—you might have to wait up to 5 minutes for a ride! The upper floors also host other activity facilities, so even on weekdays, there are quite a few people queuing for the lift.

The overall environment here is definitely not as quiet as a university library. You’ll encounter a mix of people, including uncles whose phones aren’t on silent mode and those who talk loudly on their phones.

The 3rd floor is even noisier because it houses the children’s reading area. The noise from kids running around often drifts down to the 2nd floor, making it less peaceful as well.

Sofa seating is almost always occupied—even if there’s no one sitting, personal belongings will be placed there to reserve the spot. There are usually available seats in the hard plastic chair area, though.

If you plan to spend a long time studying or reading here, you might want to bring a long-sleeved coat, as the air conditioning is set to 18°C. For residents dealing with noisy construction at home or in public spaces, this library can be a good escape. Although it’s not particularly quiet, it’s much more peaceful compared to the noise pollution in residential buildings or on the streets.

For visitors/tourists looking to take aesthetic photos, this might not be the best choice. The design of the bookshelves here isn’t as visually appealing as those in the Orchard Road Library. However, if you’re out of places to visit and just want to relax, this spot offers a glimpse into the daily lives of locals and their community atmosphere.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I visited the NLB Bedok library today to use one of the public PCs. While I was using it, the computer froze and I encountered some difficulties. I sought assistance from the staff, Miss Norsiah , who responded immediately and resolved the issue confidently and efficiently.
I would like to take this opportunity to express my sincere appreciation and thank her for her prompt, professional, and excellent assistance. Her helpfulness made my visit a pleasant experience. Once again, many thanks.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Bedok Public Library staffs is extremely kind and willing to help. It is also we'll designed from interior to exterior. I liked  the library's Teens Study and Reading Area,Early Literary Area,Multimedia Stations area and Magazine Reading Area the most I would strongly encourage you to visit this Library if you want to get BookBugs 3rd release cards as I got 35 Buwan,54 Sisik and 57 Liraes here with only 85 times.The BookBugs dispensing machine and stamp booth is at Level 3 of the librarynear the borrowing station (At level 3 main Entrance).It is also very convenient as it is at Heartbeat@Bedok, which is just 10 mins walk from EW5 Bedok station and there is lift service from level 2 to level 3 if you want to go to the early Literacy area (level 3) from the adult area (level 2) .I would like to visit this library again.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Clean and conducive atmosphere for reading and studying. Most of the spots were occupied by students who seem to be camping there , and elderly reading news paper. As a first time visitor, there are few staff to assist and level 2 entrance were closed. An impressive catalog of books (including other foreign languages ones). There seem to be seminar rooms for special program .

However, if you’re out of places to visit and just want to relax, this spot offers a glimpse into the daily lives of locals and their community atmosphere.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I visited the NLB Bedok library today to use one of the public PCs. While I was using it, the computer froze and I encountered some difficulties. I sought assistance from the staff, Miss Norsiah , who responded immediately and resolved the issue confidently and efficiently.
I would like to take this opportunity to express my sincere appreciation and thank her for her prompt, professional, and excellent assistance. Her helpfulness made my visit a pleasant experience. Once again, many thanks.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Bedok Public Library staffs is extremely kind and willing to help. It is also we'll designed from interior to exterior. I liked  the library's Teens Study and Reading Area,Early Literary Area,Multimedia Stations area and Magazine Reading Area the most I would strongly encourage you to visit this Library if you want to get BookBugs 3rd release cards as I got 35 Buwan,54 Sisik and 57 Liraes here with only 85 times.The BookBugs dispensing machine and stamp booth is at Level 3 of the librarynear the borrowing station (At level 3 main Entrance).It is also very convenient as it is at Heartbeat@Bedok, which is just 10 mins walk from EW5 Bedok station and there is lift service from level 2 to level 3 if you want to go to the early Literacy area (level 3) from the adult area (level 2) .I would like to visit this library again.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Clean and conducive atmosphere for reading and studying. Most of the spots were occupied by students who seem to be camping there , and elderly reading news paper. As a first time visitor, there are few staff to assist and level 2 entrance were closed. An impressive catalog of books (including other foreign languages ones). There seem to be seminar rooms for special program .

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Sector is optimal for mobility drills. Good vantage points, but expect heavy crosswinds. There's hawker centre nearby. Good spot to replenish calories.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
One of the best gravel experience I had and the view is not bad! Will recommend visiting it during sunrise or sunset.

Also part of PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Large park with many local residents around that area walking and jogging. Seats are available around this park. There's playground for kids as well. There's also a canoe area in a separate building nearby and it's called "Passionwave". There's toilet facilities too. Quite a number of parking space available for those who came from far. Many companies and schools like to hold their events here as the space is spacious and it is convenient to come here for the bus stops are nearby too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well maintained park with walking paths around the reservoir. The sandy path by the water takes more effort than the shady paved path running along side. A good 5km round the park. Different gradients on the paved path add to a good workout. Encountered squirrels and a lone otter too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Beautiful spot for a walk or jog! Bedok Reservoir's lush greenery and picturesque views of the water make it an ideal destination for outdoor enthusiasts. Well-maintained and clean facilities too. It’s always a nice 5 km walk !

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Weekdays is ok. Weekend is a fish market. Screaming infants and kids running around. Badly controlled during weekends due to small place. Avoid Sundays if you need a place to read.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I would like to commend the NEX Library for its attentiveness and highly proactive staff. It’s quite impressive how quickly they notice patrons—within just 30 minutes of me sitting on one of the couches, I was disturbed not once, but three times.

What stood out most was the level of dedication shown. One staff member even went the extra mile by physically slapping my chair to wake me up, followed by a firm reminder of “don’t sleep,” delivered publicly. It’s rare to see such enthusiasm in ensuring that no one gets too comfortable.

While I understand that seating is meant to be shared, I didn’t realise that briefly resting would warrant such persistent interruptions and physical gestures. It does make me wonder about the purpose of having couches in the library if taking a short rest isn’t acceptable.

Perhaps moving forward, the same level of attentiveness could be paired with a bit more courtesy and respect when approaching patrons. After all, maintaining a welcoming environment is just as important as enforcing rules.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I really like the Serangoon library because there is a specific section where there are about 15 seats alone (near the entrance) And I think that's very helpful. There also is a children's section for 10-12 year olds and a 6-8 year old section right next to the 10-12 section. They also have comics for people that like to read comics. Close to the entrance. They have this display of books and I've picked a few books from the display that I've liked so that's very nice. There is a fairly large section for Fiction and I am a Fiction fan so that's great for me. There are multiple computers there too. I don't know if it's the best place to study as a student though. But the library's location is really nice because just one level down (after you exit the library) there's a mall. I personally really like the mall because there's a Fair Price Xtra, restaurants, gadget stores and more. I wish there were more isolated seats instead of many at a certain section. The location outside of the library and mall is also nice because there is a bus station and a taxi station. Overall I would say that the Serangoon library is a very nice library to visit.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Visited three times and harassed by staff each time. They have a children’s section but their expectations for toddler behavior are unrealistic. Zero empathy. I sent my family outside just to check out our books in peace. Even then I would still get nagged with unsolicited and irrelevant advice.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Overall experience is good just that at around 4pm onwards, the library will be super crowded. They have programme room which is free seating without the need to book table but from 1pm onwards, all tables are occupied. Should add another programme room. Also the library can be very cold too

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Angela is very patient in explaining the term and conditions with me. As I’m first time in a gym with her cheerfulness make me less anxious. Thank you Edmund for explaining the detail on the package so clearly, very helpful and patient.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Fantastic 20-session journey – Highly recommend Fitness First Paya Lebar!

I recently completed an awesome 20-session journey at Fitness First Paya Lebar and cannot recommend this gym enough. The equipment is modern and kept in excellent condition, and the locker and shower facilities are good and provide everything you need for a smooth workout transition. My experience from day one was seamless; Mae provided a very professional and thorough briefing during registration, and the reception staff are consistently pleasant and welcoming. A special thank you goes to my personal trainer, Charlotte, who is truly an exceptional physical trainer and an expert in her field. She is incredibly knowledgeable and patient, taking the time to explain the proper mechanics of every machine and ensuring I understood exactly how to use them safely and effectively. Her friendly and encouraging approach made every session highly productive and enjoyable. If you are looking for a supportive environment and expert guidance to reach your fitness goals, I highly recommend.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Really grateful to have trainers like Nazree at Fitness First. He’s very experienced, knowledgeable and professional, but also friendly and easy to approach. Even when you’re not his client, he’s always happy to give advice and help out.

I used to attend his BOOM class and it was one of my favourite classes — fun, energetic and very technique-focused. Hope he brings BOOM back again!

Now attending his Hyrox class and I’m impressed by how attentive he is. He remembered my right ankle injury and always gives me options for the workout. You can really tell he cares about members’ safety and progress. .

Highly recommend Nazreen to anyone looking for a dedicated and supportive trainer! 💪

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Came here for my fitness assessment and had a great experience. The gym and lounge area were very clean and well-maintained, and the front desk staff were friendly and welcoming.

Special mention to Charlotte, who attended to me during the assessment. She was patient, friendly, and very well-versed in the exercises and movements. Even after I shared that my upper body strength was weaker, she remained encouraging and supportive throughout the session. Overall, had a really positive experience with her!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
I’ve been training at Fitness First Paya Lebar for the past two and a half months, and it has been a very positive experience.

A big part of this is thanks to my personal trainer, Charlotte. She is incredibly helpful, patient, and always fully focused during our sessions. She takes the time to ensure I’m using the correct form, explains each exercise clearly, and adjusts the workouts based on my progress and condition.

I really appreciate how attentive and encouraging she is—it makes each session both effective and enjoyable. I’ve definitely seen improvement since I started, and I feel more confident in my workouts.

Overall, I’m very happy with both the club environment and the quality of training. Highly recommended!
